1_1_purpose_and_scope.title: 1.1 Purpose and Scope
1_1_purpose_and_scope.text: This document is the canonical technical specification for the RIO Protocol, version 1.0. It defines the complete protocol requirements that a conforming implementation MUST satisfy, including all data structures, cryptographic operations, pipeline stage behaviors, invariants, and verification procedures. An external engineering team reading only this document, with no access to the reference implementation source code or to the original authors, MUST be able to implement a fully RIO-compliant system.
covers: The Three-Loop Architecture that governs all RIO execution
All data structures, schemas, and field-level constraints
All cryptographic algorithms, parameters, and exact encoding rules
All verification procedures, including independent third-party verification
Protocol invariants that MUST never be violated
does_not_cover: Implementation-specific details such as programming language, framework, or module structure
API endpoint paths, HTTP transport mechanics, or deployment infrastructure
Dashboard, web UI, or client-side signing tool specifications
Advanced governance analytics or learning-loop machine-learning components
The full threat model (summarized in Section 9; a complete threat analysis is maintained separately)

1_2_canonical_definition.title: 1.2 Canonical Definition
1_2_canonical_definition.text: RIO is a governed execution system that sits between AI models, human principals, and real-world actions. It translates natural-language goals into structured, verifiable intents; evaluates those intents against policy and risk criteria; requires explicit approval when authorization thresholds are met; controls execution through a cryptographically enforced hard gate; verifies outcomes against the approved intent; and generates signed, chained receipts recorded in a tamper-evident ledger. The system enforces the rules. The AI does not.
1_2_canonical_definition.founding_principle: No AI model can cause an action to execute without passing through the Sovereign Gate (cryptographic authentication) and the Execution Gate (cryptographic authorization). Both gates are fail-closed. Every execution, whether permitted or blocked, is recorded in the audit ledger.

1_3_terminology.title: 1.3 Terminology and Definitions
1_3_terminology.rfc_2119_note: The key words MUST, MUST NOT, REQUIRED, SHALL, SHALL NOT, SHOULD, SHOULD NOT, RECOMMENDED, MAY, and OPTIONAL are to be interpreted as described in RFC 2119.

terms.definition: A real-world operation executed by the gateway on behalf of an approved intent. In this implementation, the action is always an AI model invocation.
The first 16 hexadecimal characters of SHA-256(RIO_PUBLIC_KEY_raw_bytes). Identifies the cryptographic authority that authorized an execution.
The intent string after grammar normalization and optional context injection. This is the string passed to the AI model.
The deterministic byte encoding of gate parameters used as the ECDSA signing input for execution tokens. Formula: UTF-8_encode(intent + '|' + source + '|' + timestamp).
The SHA-256 seal of a single execution_ledger row. Each entry's hash becomes the prev_hash of the next entry, forming the integrity chain.
The inner hard gate (check_gate) that enforces a five-guard validation sequence before any AI model invocation.
A short-lived, base64-encoded DER ECDSA signature over the canonical gate parameters. Proves server-side authorization of a specific execution.
The literal ASCII string 'GENESIS', used as the prev_hash or prev_ledger_hash for the first row in any hash chain.
A structured object returned by check_gate on successful gate passage. Contains receipt_hash, intent_hash, parameters_hash, approver, and related fields.
Execution that has passed through all required pipeline stages, including both the Sovereign Gate and the Execution Gate.
A natural-language string expressing what the caller wants the AI to do. The fundamental unit of input to the RIO Pipeline.
SHA-256(UTF-8_encode(intent_string)). Used to index the intent across ledger tables without storing the raw intent text.
See Approver.
The SHA-256 seal of a single post_execution_ledger row. Used as the HMAC input for that row's integrity signature.
A caller-supplied, single-use random string included in the intake request to prevent replay attacks at the intent level.
SHA-256 of the canonical bytes encoding of gate parameters. Cross-references execution_ledger and post_execution_ledger entries for the same request.
In the in-memory ledger: SHA-256 of the pipe-delimited concatenation of ledger entry fields. In the execution ledger: the SHA-256 seal of a gate-passage event.
The ECDSA secp256k1 private signing key. Used to sign intents and generate execution tokens. MUST never be logged or returned to callers.
The ECDSA secp256k1 public verifying key. Used to verify all signatures. Set as an environment variable in the gateway.
A shared secret used as the HMAC key for post-execution ledger entry signatures.
An alternative Bearer token accepted by service-protected endpoints. Functionally equivalent to RIO_SERVICE_TOKEN for authentication purposes.
SHA-256 of the raw DER signature bytes. Used as the primary key in the Signature Registry to enforce per-signature single-use.
The outermost cryptographic checkpoint. Performs ECDSA signature verification on the raw intent string before any processing begins.
A caller-supplied string identifying the origin system of the request (e.g., 'manus', 'cli', 'web').
Time-to-live. Defaults to SIGNATURE_WINDOW_SECONDS (default: 300 seconds).

1_4_notation_conventions.title: 1.4 Notation Conventions
1_4_notation_conventions.pseudocode: Algorithm steps use language-agnostic pseudocode. Function calls use FUNCTION_NAME(arguments). String concatenation uses +. The pipe separator | refers to the literal ASCII character U+007C.
1_4_notation_conventions.hash_notation: SHA-256(x) denotes the SHA-256 digest of byte sequence x, returned as a lowercase hexadecimal string unless otherwise noted. UTF-8_encode(s) denotes the UTF-8 byte encoding of string s.
1_4_notation_conventions.base64: Base64_encode(b) is standard Base64 encoding (RFC 4648, alphabet A-Za-z0-9+/, with = padding). Base64_decode(s) is the inverse.
1_4_notation_conventions.slicing: x[:n] denotes the first n characters of string x.
1_4_notation_conventions.field_tables: Fields are listed with: Name, Type, Required/Optional, Constraints, and Description.
1_4_notation_conventions.section_references: Cross-references use the form (see Section N.N).

2_1_three_loop_architecture.title: 2.1 Three-Loop Architecture
2_1_three_loop_architecture.description: The RIO Protocol organizes all activity into three structurally separated loops. Each loop has distinct responsibilities, data flows, and trust requirements. Separation between loops is a protocol invariant (INV-07): no loop may bypass the responsibilities of another.

loops.name: Intake and Discovery Loop
loops.responsibility: Accept raw goals from callers, translate them into structured intents, verify the caller's cryptographic identity, and determine whether the intent may proceed.
data_flow_in: ECDSA signature over the intent (signed by the caller using RIO_PRIVATE_KEY)
Timestamp and optional nonce (for replay protection)
Optional model preference and payload context
data_flow_out: Replay status (nonce and signature registry check results)
loops.trust_boundary: The Intake Loop validates that the request originates from an authorized caller. It does NOT grant execution rights. Execution rights are granted only by the Execution Gate in Loop 2.

loops.name: Execution and Governance Loop
loops.responsibility: Enforce the hard execution gate, execute the governed action (AI model invocation), capture the outcome, generate the signed receipt, and record both the gate decision and the execution outcome in the tamper-evident audit ledger.
data_flow_in: Execution token (ECDSA signature over canonical gate parameters)
Gate receipt (returned by check_gate on success)
data_flow_out: Execution ledger entries (gate decision and post-execution outcome)
loops.trust_boundary: The Execution Gate in Loop 2 is the last line of defense. Even if Loop 1 somehow passed a malformed or unauthorized intent, the Execution Gate verifies the execution token independently using the same ECDSA key. Failure at this boundary is fail-closed: no action executes.

loops.name: Learning and Audit Loop
loops.responsibility: Maintain the persistent audit record, enable retrospective verification of all past decisions and outcomes, support chain integrity verification, and provide the audit trail for governance review.
data_flow_in: Execution ledger entries (one per gate decision)
Post-execution ledger entries (one per completed execution)
In-memory response ledger entries (one per successful response)
data_flow_out: Per-intent audit trails (gate entries + post-execution entries for a single intent)
loops.trust_boundary: Loop 3 is read-only from the perspective of callers. The audit ledger is append-only; existing entries MUST NOT be modified after recording.

2_2_eight_stage_pipeline.title: 2.2 Eight-Stage Governed Execution Pipeline
2_2_eight_stage_pipeline.description: Every intake request traverses exactly eight stages in strict sequential order. A failure at any stage is fail-closed: the request is rejected immediately with no advancement to subsequent stages and no AI model invocation.

stages.name: Receipt Generation and In-Memory Ledger Commit
Post-Execution Audit Ledger Recording
stages.fail_behavior: Non-failing, asynchronous; write errors are logged

2_3_trust_boundaries.title: 2.4 Trust Boundaries

boundaries.name: Intake Boundary
boundaries.crossing_mechanism: Valid ECDSA signature over intent + timestamp within window

boundaries.name: Signature Registry Boundary
boundaries.crossing_mechanism: Atomic DB claim of SHA-256(signature_bytes) — single-use

boundaries.name: Execution Boundary
boundaries.crossing_mechanism: Valid ECDSA execution token over canonical gate parameters

boundaries.name: Audit Boundary
boundaries.crossing_mechanism: Append-only writes only; no modification path exists

2_3_trust_boundaries.constraint: No request MAY cross TB-3 without having successfully crossed TB-1 and TB-2 in the same request lifecycle.

2_4_fail_closed_principle.title: 2.5 Fail-Closed Principle
rules: Default deny. No AI model executes unless every required verification step explicitly passes.
Infrastructure failures deny. If any cryptographic verification component is unavailable (key not loaded, database unreachable), the request MUST be blocked. There is no fail-open fallback.
Concurrent replay denial. Concurrent requests with the same signature MUST both be denied. The atomic claim mechanism enforces this.
Audit regardless of outcome. Every gate decision — pass or block — MUST be written to the execution ledger. An unaudited execution is a protocol violation.
Async isolation. The post-execution ledger write (Stage 8) MUST NOT delay the caller response, but its failure MUST be logged.

stage_1_intake.title: 3.1 Stage 1: Intent Intake and Translation
stage_1_intake.purpose: Accept the raw intake request, compute the signature hash, perform an atomic single-use claim in the Signature Registry, and detect any signature-level replay before cryptographic verification begins.

inputs.constraint: REQUIRED. Base64-encoded DER ECDSA signature bytes.
REQUIRED. The raw natural-language intent string.
Passed through; not inspected in this stage.

processing_steps: 1. Base64-decode the signature field to obtain raw_sig_bytes.
2. Compute sig_hash = SHA-256(raw_sig_bytes).

3. Perform an atomic check-and-insert against the used_signatures table: (a) Query for existing row where signature_hash = sig_hash; (b) Evict rows where expires_at <= Unix_epoch_now(); (c) If found: replay — reject; (d) If not found: INSERT with expires_at = Unix_epoch_now() + SIGNATURE_WINDOW_SECONDS; (e) If INSERT raises uniqueness constraint error: concurrent replay — reject; (f) Any other DB error: block (fail-closed).
4. On successful claim: record sig_hash for inclusion in the final response.

outputs.description: SHA-256 of raw signature bytes. Included in the intake response as signature_hash.
Row in used_signatures with TTL set.

stage_2_sovereign_gate.title: 3.2 Stage 2: Sovereign Gate — Cryptographic Authentication
stage_2_sovereign_gate.purpose: Verify that the intent was cryptographically signed by the holder of RIO_PRIVATE_KEY, that the request is within the valid time window, and that any nonce has not been previously consumed.

inputs.constraint: REQUIRED. The raw intent string (the signed message).
OPTIONAL but STRONGLY RECOMMENDED. Single-use token.

checks.name: Key Availability
checks.procedure: Attempt to load RIO_PUBLIC_KEY from the environment.

checks.name: Timestamp Freshness
checks.procedure: Parse timestamp as ISO-8601 UTC. Verify abs(UTC_now - timestamp) <= SIGNATURE_WINDOW_SECONDS.

checks.name: ECDSA Verification
checks.procedure: Verify the decoded signature bytes against UTF-8_encode(intent) using RIO_PUBLIC_KEY, curve secp256k1, hash SHA-256, DER encoding.

checks.name: Nonce Claim
checks.procedure: If nonce is present: evict expired nonces, check registry, claim if not present. Absence of nonce is accepted with reduced replay-protection guarantees.

checks.name: Accept
checks.procedure: Log acceptance. Pipeline proceeds to Stage 3.

stage_3_execution_gate.title: 3.3 Stage 3: Execution Gate — Authorization Verification
stage_3_execution_gate.purpose: Enforce the hard execution gate (check_gate) using a five-guard sequence. The final barrier before any AI model invocation. Every call — pass or block — MUST be recorded in the execution_ledger.

inputs.constraint: REQUIRED. Same value used in the execution token.
REQUIRED. Base64-encoded DER ECDSA signature over canonical bytes.

guards.name: Required Fields
guards.procedure: Check that intent, source, and timestamp are all present and non-empty.

guards.name: Timestamp Freshness
guards.procedure: Parse timestamp as ISO-8601 UTC. Verify abs(UTC_now - timestamp) <= SIGNATURE_WINDOW_SECONDS.

guards.name: Token Presence
guards.procedure: Verify execution_token is present and non-empty.

guards.name: ECDSA Signature
guards.procedure: Base64-decode execution_token. Verify signature bytes against canonical_bytes using RIO_PUBLIC_KEY, secp256k1, SHA-256, DER.

stage_4_grammar_calibration.title: 3.4 Stage 4: Grammar Calibration and Semantic Normalization
stage_4_grammar_calibration.purpose: Normalize the intent string and optionally augment it with additional context before routing and AI model invocation.
processing_steps: 1. Trim leading and trailing whitespace from intent: calibrated = trim(intent)
2. If payload.context is present and non-empty: context_truncated = trim(payload.context)[:300]; calibrated = calibrated + '\n\n[Context: ' + context_truncated + ']'
3. Context value MUST be truncated to 300 characters before appending.
4. Result is calibrated_intent — passed to the AI model in Stage 6.
stage_4_grammar_calibration.failure_conditions: None. This stage does not fail.
invariants_enforced: Calibrated intent MUST be a deterministic function of raw intent and payload context.

stage_5_model_routing.title: 3.5 Stage 5: Model Routing and Selection
stage_5_model_routing.purpose: Determine which AI model will handle the calibrated intent, based on explicit caller preference or intent keyword analysis.

step_1_explicit_keywords.description: If model field is present and non-empty, normalize to lowercase and apply keyword matching:
step_1_explicit_keywords.fallthrough: If no keyword matches, proceed to step 2.

step_2_intent_keywords.description: Apply intent keyword routing (case-insensitive match against calibrated_intent):

outputs.description: One of 'claude', 'chatgpt', 'gemini'. Included in the intake response.

stage_5_model_routing.failure_conditions: None. Default model ('claude') ensures this stage never fails.

stage_6_execution.title: 3.6 Stage 6: Governed Model Execution
stage_6_execution.purpose: Invoke the selected AI model with the calibrated intent. Only reached after Stages 1-3 have all passed.
processing_steps: 1. Select the AI client based on model_used.
2. Invoke the model with calibrated_intent as the sole user message. Parameters: max_tokens=1024 (for Claude and ChatGPT); model string as specified in Stage 5 table.
3. Capture the complete text response as ai_response.
4. If the model call raises any exception, return HTTP 502. Log the error. Pipeline terminates.

outputs.description: Complete text response from the AI model.

stage_6_execution.note: After ai_response is captured, Stage 8 is initiated asynchronously. Stage 6 does not wait for Stage 8 to complete.

stage_7_receipt.title: 3.7 Stage 7: Receipt Generation and In-Memory Ledger Commit
stage_7_receipt.purpose: Generate the integrity-sealed receipt for this execution and commit it to the in-memory hash-chain ledger.

inputs.constraint: REQUIRED. receipt_hash of the previous in-memory ledger entry, or 'GENESIS' for the first.

processing_steps: 1. Compute response_ts = UTC_now as ISO-8601 string.
2. Compute receipt_hash: data = prev_hash + '|' + source + '|' + intent + '|' + model_used + '|' + ai_response[:500] + '|' + response_ts; receipt_hash = SHA-256(UTF-8_encode(data)).
3. Only the first 500 characters of ai_response are included in the hash computation.
4. Compute intent_hash = SHA-256(UTF-8_encode(intent)).
5. Atomically (under a mutex/lock) append the entry to the in-memory ledger. Increment sequential ledger_index (starting from 1). Set self._prev_hash = receipt_hash for the next append.
6. Return ledger_index and receipt_hash for inclusion in the HTTP response.

outputs.description: SHA-256 seal of this ledger entry. Included in the intake response.
Sequential 1-based position in the in-memory ledger. Included in the intake response.

stage_8_post_exec_ledger.title: 3.8 Stage 8: Post-Execution Audit Ledger Recording
stage_8_post_exec_ledger.purpose: Append a tamper-evident, HMAC-signed entry to the post_execution_ledger table. Runs asynchronously; MUST NOT delay the HTTP response.

inputs.constraint: REQUIRED. ledger_hash of the previous row, or 'GENESIS'.

processing_steps: 1. Compute ts = UTC_now as ISO-8601 string.
2. Compute result_hash = SHA-256(UTF-8_encode(ai_response)).
3. Fetch prev_ledger_hash: query last ledger_hash in post_execution_ledger ORDER BY id DESC LIMIT 1. If empty, use 'GENESIS'.
4. Set policy_result = 'success'.

5. Compute ledger_hash: seal_data = ts + '|' + approver + '|' + model_used + '|' + source + '|' + policy_result + '|' + parameters_hash + '|' + result_hash + '|' + prev_ledger_hash; ledger_hash = SHA-256(UTF-8_encode(seal_data)).
6. Compute HMAC: hmac_key = UTF-8_encode(RIO_SERVICE_TOKEN); signature = HMAC-SHA256(key=hmac_key, msg=UTF-8_encode(ledger_hash)). If RIO_SERVICE_TOKEN not set: signature = 'key_unavailable'.
7. Insert row into post_execution_ledger.
8. Log success or failure. Write failures MUST NOT surface to the caller.
stage_8_post_exec_ledger.failure_conditions: Write failures are logged but silent to the caller.

fields.name: source
fields.description: Caller identifier. Stored as executed_by in ledger tables.

fields.name: intent
fields.description: Natural-language instruction for the AI. The text that is cryptographically signed.

fields.name: signature
fields.constraints: Valid Base64; decodes to DER ECDSA bytes
fields.description: ECDSA secp256k1/SHA-256/DER signature over UTF-8_encode(intent). Base64-encoded.

fields.name: timestamp
fields.constraints: ISO-8601 UTC; within SIGNATURE_WINDOW_SECONDS of server time
fields.description: Time at which the signature was produced. MUST match the timestamp used when generating the execution_token.

fields.name: model
fields.description: Preferred model. One of 'claude', 'chatgpt', 'gemini', or a string containing their keywords.

fields.name: payload
fields.description: payload.context (string, <=300 chars after truncation) is injected into the calibrated intent.

fields.name: metadata
fields.description: Caller-supplied metadata. Stored but not processed by the pipeline.

fields.name: nonce
fields.constraints: Non-empty if present; unique per request
fields.description: Single-use token preventing intent-level replay. Consumed by the Nonce Registry.

fields.name: execution_token
fields.constraints: Valid Base64; decodes to DER ECDSA bytes
fields.description: Short-lived ECDSA signature over canonical gate parameters. MUST be generated immediately before the request using RIO_PRIVATE_KEY.

example.intent: Summarise the key properties of the secp256k1 elliptic curve.

fields.name: status
fields.description: Always 'success' on HTTP 200.

fields.name: model_used
fields.description: Model identifier: 'claude', 'chatgpt', or 'gemini'.

fields.name: response
fields.description: Complete text response from the AI model.

fields.name: signature_verified
fields.description: Always true on HTTP 200.

fields.name: signature_hash
fields.description: SHA-256 of the raw DER signature bytes. Primary key in used_signatures.

fields.name: receipt_hash
fields.description: SHA-256 seal of the in-memory ledger entry for this execution.

fields.name: ledger_index
fields.description: Sequential 1-based position in the in-memory ledger.

fields.name: timestamp
fields.description: ISO-8601 UTC timestamp of when the response was produced.

4_3_execution_token.title: 4.3 Execution Token Schema

fields.name: token
fields.description: The execution token. Base64-encoded DER ECDSA signature over canonical_bytes.

fields.name: timestamp
fields.description: ISO-8601 UTC timestamp used in canonical encoding. THIS value MUST be used unchanged as the timestamp field of the intake request.

fields.name: parameters_hash
fields.description: SHA-256 of canonical_bytes. Stored in both ledger tables as a cross-reference key.

4_3_execution_token.critical_constraint: The timestamp field returned in the execution token object MUST be used verbatim as the timestamp field of the subsequent intake request. Any modification causes canonical encoding mismatch and Guard 5 will reject the token.

fields.name: gate_verified
fields.description: Always true when returned (failures raise exceptions).

fields.name: receipt_hash
fields.description: SHA-256 of the gate passage event string.

fields.name: intent_hash
fields.description: SHA-256(UTF-8_encode(intent)).

fields.name: source
fields.description: The source field from gate parameters.

fields.name: timestamp
fields.description: ISO-8601 UTC of gate passage.

fields.name: parameters_hash
fields.description: SHA-256 of canonical bytes. Cross-reference key in both ledger tables.

fields.name: approver
fields.description: Key fingerprint: first 16 hex chars of SHA-256(RIO_PUBLIC_KEY_raw_bytes).

4_5_in_memory_ledger_entry.title: 4.5 In-Memory Ledger Entry Schema
4_5_in_memory_ledger_entry.note: Process-lifetime only. Does not survive restarts.

fields.name: ledger_index
fields.description: Sequential 1-based position.

fields.name: receipt_hash
fields.description: SHA-256 seal of this entry. Becomes prev_hash of the next entry.

fields.name: prev_hash
fields.description: receipt_hash of the immediately preceding entry. 'GENESIS' for the first entry.

fields.name: source
fields.description: Caller identifier.

fields.name: intent_hash
fields.description: SHA-256(UTF-8_encode(intent)).

fields.name: model
fields.description: Model identifier used for execution.

fields.name: response_chars
fields.description: Character length of the AI response.

fields.name: timestamp
fields.description: ISO-8601 UTC of the ledger commit.

4_6_execution_ledger.title: 4.6 Execution Ledger Schema (SQLite — gateway.db)
4_6_execution_ledger.note: One row written per check_gate invocation, regardless of outcome.

columns.description: Row identifier. Determines chain order.
Always 'check_gate'.
AI model identifier, or empty string if not resolved at gate time.
Key fingerprint: first 16 hex chars of SHA-256(RIO_PUBLIC_KEY_raw). 'key_unavailable' if key not set.
Value of source from the intake request.
SHA-256(UTF-8_encode(intent)).
SHA-256(canonical_bytes). Cross-reference to post_execution_ledger.
'executed' if gate passed; 'blocked' if any guard failed.
'gate_passed' on success; a reason code string on failure.
Gate receipt hash on success; empty string on failure.
entry_hash of the previous row by id, or 'GENESIS' for the first row.
SHA-256 seal of this row.
ISO-8601 UTC when the row was written.

4_7_post_execution_ledger.title: 4.7 Post-Execution Ledger Schema (SQLite — gateway.db)
4_7_post_execution_ledger.note: One row written asynchronously after each successful AI execution.

columns.description: Row identifier. Determines chain order.
ISO-8601 UTC when the AI execution completed.
Key fingerprint: first 16 hex chars of SHA-256(RIO_PUBLIC_KEY_raw).
AI model string (e.g., 'claude-sonnet-4-6').
Value of source from the intake request.
'success' on successful execution; 'error:<message>' on error.
SHA-256(canonical_bytes). Cross-reference to execution_ledger.
SHA-256(UTF-8_encode(ai_response)).
SHA-256 chain seal of this entry.
HMAC-SHA256(ledger_hash, RIO_SERVICE_TOKEN). 'key_unavailable' if token not set.
SHA-256(Base64_decode(signature)). The deduplication key.
The raw intent text associated with this signature (for audit).
ISO-8601 UTC timestamp of first use.
Unix epoch float. Row MAY be evicted when expires_at <= Unix_now.
Row identifier.
'gate_passed' or 'unauthorized execution attempt'.
SHA-256(UTF-8_encode(intent)).
Caller identifier.
Gate receipt hash on success; empty string on failure.
ISO-8601 UTC when the event occurred.

algorithms.parameters: Curve: secp256k1; Hash: SHA-256; Encoding: DER

supported_formats.detection: Contains '-----BEGIN PUBLIC KEY-----' with embedded newlines
Contains '-----BEGIN PUBLIC KEY-----' but line breaks replaced with spaces
supported_formats.normalization: Extract body; strip all whitespace; re-wrap at 64 chars per line; reconstruct PEM.

supported_formats.detection: No PEM markers; Base64 characters only
supported_formats.normalization: Strip whitespace; re-wrap at 64 chars; prepend/append PEM markers.

supported_formats.detection: Hex string, length 66 (compressed) or 130 (uncompressed) characters after stripping 0x
supported_formats.normalization: Decode hex to bytes; load as raw secp256k1 point.

public_key.fail_behavior: If RIO_PUBLIC_KEY is not set or cannot be parsed: all requests MUST be blocked with HTTP 503.

private_key.security_requirement: The private key MUST NEVER be logged, included in any response body, or returned to any caller.

supported_formats.detection: Contains '-----BEGIN EC PRIVATE KEY-----' with embedded newlines
Contains '-----BEGIN' but line breaks replaced with spaces

5_2_key_material.key_pairing: RIO_PUBLIC_KEY and RIO_PRIVATE_KEY MUST form a valid secp256k1 key pair.

steps: Step 1: message_bytes = UTF-8_encode(intent_string)
Step 2: (r, s) = ECDSA_sign(message_bytes, key=RIO_PRIVATE_KEY, curve=secp256k1, hash=SHA-256)
Step 3: sig_bytes = DER_encode(r, s)
Step 4: signature_b64 = Base64_encode(sig_bytes)
Step 5: timestamp = UTC_now formatted as ISO-8601
5_3_intent_signature_construction.output: (signature_b64, timestamp). Both MUST be included in the intake request.
5_3_intent_signature_construction.der_encoding: ASN.1 DER SEQUENCE of two INTEGERs (r, s): 0x30 [total_length] 0x02 [r_length] [r_bytes] 0x02 [s_length] [s_bytes]. If the high bit of the first byte of r or s is set, prepend 0x00 to that integer.

steps: Step 1: timestamp = UTC_now formatted as ISO-8601 (MUST be used verbatim in intake request)
Step 2: canonical_str = intent + '|' + source + '|' + timestamp
Step 3: canonical_bytes = UTF-8_encode(canonical_str)
Step 4: sig_bytes = ECDSA_sign(canonical_bytes, key=RIO_PRIVATE_KEY, curve=secp256k1, hash=SHA-256, encoding=DER)
Step 5: token = Base64_encode(sig_bytes)
Step 6: parameters_hash = SHA-256(canonical_bytes)
canonical_encoding_rules: Fields: intent, source, timestamp in that exact order.
Separator: the pipe character | (U+007C). Not URL-encoded. Not escaped.
Encoding: UTF-8 bytes of the concatenated string.
No trailing separator. No leading whitespace. No newlines.
verification_steps: Step 3: result = ECDSA_verify(sig_bytes, canonical_bytes, key=RIO_PUBLIC_KEY, curve=secp256k1, hash=SHA-256, decoding=DER)
Step 4: BadSignatureError -> block (invalid_signature); other error -> block (verification_error); success -> proceed

universal_rules: 1. Encode the input string to bytes using UTF-8.
3. Return the result as a 64-character lowercase hexadecimal string.

specific_hashes.name: signature_hash
specific_hashes.note: Input is raw bytes, not a string.

5_6_execution_ledger_entry_hash.formula: entry_hash = SHA-256(UTF-8_encode(action + '|' + agent + '|' + approver + '|' + executed_by + '|' + intent_id + '|' + parameters_hash + '|' + result + '|' + reason + '|' + receipt_hash + '|' + prev_hash + '|' + timestamp))
critical_notes: Fields MUST be concatenated in exactly the order shown.
For a 'blocked' entry, receipt_hash is an empty string; the | separator and empty string MUST still be present in seal_data.
Two consecutive || appears in blocked entries (agent is empty AND receipt_hash is empty).
5_6_execution_ledger_entry_hash.chain_link_rule: The prev_hash field of row N MUST equal the entry_hash of the row with id = N-1. The row with the smallest id MUST have prev_hash = 'GENESIS'.

5_7_post_exec_ledger_hash_and_hmac.step_2_prev_ledger_hash: prev_ledger_hash = ledger_hash of the most recent row by id, OR 'GENESIS' if the table is empty.

step_3_ledger_hash.formula: ledger_hash = SHA-256(UTF-8_encode(timestamp + '|' + approver + '|' + agent + '|' + executed_by + '|' + policy_result + '|' + parameters_hash + '|' + result_hash + '|' + prev_ledger_hash))

step_4_hmac_signature.fallback: If RIO_SERVICE_TOKEN is not configured: signature = 'key_unavailable'

5_7_post_exec_ledger_hash_and_hmac.chain_link_rule: The prev_ledger_hash input MUST equal the ledger_hash stored in the immediately preceding row by id. The first row uses 'GENESIS'.

5_8_in_memory_receipt_hash.formula: receipt_hash = SHA-256(UTF-8_encode(prev_hash + '|' + source + '|' + intent + '|' + model_used + '|' + ai_response[:500] + '|' + timestamp))
5_8_in_memory_receipt_hash.truncation_rule: Only the first 500 characters of ai_response are included.
5_8_in_memory_receipt_hash.chain_link_rule: The first entry uses prev_hash = 'GENESIS'. Each subsequent entry's prev_hash is the receipt_hash of the immediately preceding entry.

5_9_signature_registry_fingerprint.note: The input to SHA-256 is the raw DER-encoded ECDSA bytes, not the Base64 representation.

5_10_key_fingerprint.fallback: If RIO_PUBLIC_KEY is not set: approver = 'key_unavailable'

5_11_encoding_rules.encoding: Lowercase hexadecimal string, 64 characters for SHA-256
Lowercase hexadecimal string, 64 characters for HMAC-SHA256
Literal ASCII | (U+007C); not URL-encoded, not backslash-escaped

checks.name: Key Availability
checks.procedure: Confirm RIO_PUBLIC_KEY is present and parseable.

checks.name: Timestamp Freshness
checks.procedure: Parse timestamp as ISO-8601 UTC. Compute delta = abs(verify_time - timestamp).

checks.name: Signature Presence
checks.procedure: Confirm signature field is present and non-empty.

checks.name: ECDSA Validity
checks.procedure: Verify decoded signature bytes against UTF-8_encode(intent) using RIO_PUBLIC_KEY, secp256k1, SHA-256, DER.

checks.name: Nonce Status
checks.procedure: Determine whether nonce (if present) was first use or replay.

checks.name: Registry Status
checks.procedure: Determine whether SHA-256(Base64_decode(signature)) appears in used_signatures.
checks.pass_criterion: For gatekeeping: must not be present. For post-hoc audit: must be present.

steps: Step 1: canonical_bytes = UTF-8_encode(intent + '|' + source + '|' + timestamp)
Step 2: sig_bytes = Base64_decode(execution_token)
Step 3: result = ECDSA_verify(sig_bytes, canonical_bytes, key=RIO_PUBLIC_KEY, curve=secp256k1, hash=SHA-256, decoding=DER)
Step 4: Confirm timestamp is within window (for real-time verification).
Step 5: Confirm intent, source, and timestamp are all non-empty.
Step 1: Retrieve all rows from execution_ledger ORDER BY id ASC.
Step 2: Initialize prev = 'GENESIS'
Step 3: For each row: (a) Recompute entry_hash from row fields using Section 5.6 formula; (b) Verify computed == row.entry_hash; (c) Verify row.prev_hash == prev; (d) Set prev = row.entry_hash
Step 4: If any check fails: chain is NOT intact. Report first failure.
Step 5: If all checks pass: chain is intact.
Step 1: Retrieve all rows from post_execution_ledger ORDER BY id ASC.
Step 2: Initialize prev_ledger_hash = 'GENESIS'

Step 3: For each row: (a) Recompute ledger_hash from row fields using Section 5.7 formula; (b) Verify computed == row.ledger_hash; (c) Verify chain linkage; (d) Recompute HMAC and verify == row.signature; (e) Set prev_ledger_hash = row.ledger_hash
Step 4: Report per-row results.

verifiable_properties: Every gate passage receipt hash is correctly computed.
Every chain link in execution_ledger is valid.
Every ledger_hash in post_execution_ledger is correctly computed.
Every HMAC signature in post_execution_ledger matches.
6_5_independent_third_party_verification.not_required: The verifier does NOT need access to the original intent strings, the AI responses, or any runtime process.

7_1_protocol_invariants.name: All execution is cryptographically authenticated.
7_1_protocol_invariants.description: No AI model invocation MAY proceed without a valid ECDSA secp256k1 signature from the holder of RIO_PRIVATE_KEY over the exact intent string.

7_1_protocol_invariants.name: Signatures are single-use.
7_1_protocol_invariants.description: A given ECDSA signature (identified by SHA-256(raw_DER_bytes)) MUST be accepted exactly once. Replays MUST be rejected with HTTP 409 regardless of whether the signature is cryptographically valid.

7_1_protocol_invariants.name: No execution without execution gate authorization.
7_1_protocol_invariants.description: No AI model MAY be invoked without a valid execution token verified by check_gate. Passing the Sovereign Gate alone is insufficient.

7_1_protocol_invariants.name: Hash chains are append-only and monotonically growing.
7_1_protocol_invariants.description: Entries MUST be appended in order. prev_hash of row N MUST equal entry_hash of row N-1. No existing entry MAY be modified or deleted.

7_1_protocol_invariants.name: Every gate decision is audited.
7_1_protocol_invariants.description: Every invocation of check_gate — 'executed' or 'blocked' — MUST produce a row in execution_ledger AND a row in gate_log.

7_1_protocol_invariants.name: Infrastructure failures close the gate.
7_1_protocol_invariants.description: If any verification infrastructure component raises an error, the request MUST be blocked. No execution MAY proceed when verification cannot be completed.

7_1_protocol_invariants.name: Loop separation is maintained.
7_1_protocol_invariants.description: Loop 1 MUST complete before Loop 2 begins. The Execution Gate MUST be the last checkpoint before model invocation. Loop 3 writes MUST occur after and independently of the HTTP response.

7_1_protocol_invariants.name: Every governed execution has a receipt.
7_1_protocol_invariants.description: Every successful AI model execution MUST produce an in-memory ledger entry with a receipt hash, and MUST initiate a post-execution ledger write.

7_2_system_invariants.invariant: The key pair (RIO_PRIVATE_KEY, RIO_PUBLIC_KEY) MUST be a valid secp256k1 pair. The private key MUST NEVER appear in any log, response, or exported data.
Ledger rows MUST be ordered by insertion time. id AUTOINCREMENT values MUST be monotonically increasing.
Only the holder of RIO_PRIVATE_KEY can produce valid intake signatures and valid execution tokens.
The union of execution_ledger entries and gate_log entries constitutes the complete record of all gate decisions.
The default state of the system, in the absence of a valid, verified intake request, is to deny execution.
The approver field on every ledger entry identifies the authority that authorized execution. A change in approver value indicates a key rotation.

7_4_emergency_kill_switch_eks0.mechanism: Remove or invalidate RIO_PUBLIC_KEY from the environment. Immediately, all incoming requests will fail at Stage 2, Check 1 (HTTP 503) AND Stage 3, Guard 4 (HTTP 403).
7_4_emergency_kill_switch_eks0.audit_during_eks0: All blocked attempts are recorded in execution_ledger with result='blocked' and reason='key_unavailable' or 'key_not_configured'.
required_properties: Immediate effect: Takes effect on the next request; no process restart required.
Non-destructive: Does not delete or modify any audit data.
Reversible: Restoring RIO_PUBLIC_KEY re-enables the system.
Auditable: All blocked attempts during the kill switch period are recorded.
7_4_emergency_kill_switch_eks0.conformance_requirement: Conforming implementations MUST support an equivalent kill switch mechanism that satisfies all four properties above.

section_8_conformance.title: 8. Conformance Requirements

8_1_conformance_levels.name: Cryptographic Compliance
8_1_conformance_levels.summary: Produces correct cryptographic artifacts: valid signatures, correct hash computations, correct ledger entry sealing.

8_1_conformance_levels.name: Pipeline Compliance
8_1_conformance_levels.summary: Implements all eight pipeline stages in order, enforces all eight protocol invariants, produces execution ledger entries for every gate decision.

8_1_conformance_levels.name: Full Protocol Compliance
8_1_conformance_levels.summary: Level 2 plus Three-Loop Architecture separation, asynchronous post-execution audit, independent verification support, and kill switch capability.

8_2_level_1_must: Use ECDSA secp256k1 with SHA-256 and DER encoding for all asymmetric signature operations.
Produce intent signatures verifiable with ECDSA_verify(sig_bytes, UTF-8_encode(intent), RIO_PUBLIC_KEY, secp256k1, SHA-256, DER).
Produce execution tokens verifiable with the same procedure applied to UTF-8_encode(intent + '|' + source + '|' + timestamp).
Compute parameters_hash as SHA-256(UTF-8_encode(intent + '|' + source + '|' + timestamp)).
Compute entry_hash exactly as specified in Section 5.6.
Compute ledger_hash and HMAC signature exactly as specified in Section 5.7.
Compute in-memory receipt_hash exactly as specified in Section 5.8.
Use 'GENESIS' as the initial prev_hash / prev_ledger_hash for the first row in any chain.
Produce all hash values as lowercase 64-character hexadecimal strings.
8_3_level_2_must: Implement all eight pipeline stages in the exact order defined in Section 3.
Block execution and return an appropriate error at each stage's defined failure conditions.
Implement the Signature Registry with atomic check-and-insert semantics, persistent storage, and TTL-based eviction.
Implement the Sovereign Gate with all six checks in order.
Implement the Execution Gate (check_gate) with all five guards in order.
Write both a gate_log entry and an execution_ledger entry for every check_gate invocation.
Compute and store prev_hash / entry_hash correctly for every execution_ledger entry.
Write a post_execution_ledger entry for every successful AI model execution.
Include receipt_hash, ledger_index, signature_hash, model_used, signature_verified, and timestamp in the HTTP 200 response.
8_4_level_3_must: Three-Loop Architecture: Structurally separate Intake Loop (Stages 1-2, 4-5), Execution Loop (Stage 3, 6-7), and Audit Loop (Stage 8).
Asynchronous Audit: Stage 8 MUST be initiated as a non-blocking fire-and-forget task.
Independent Verification Support: Expose interfaces allowing external verification with only RIO_PUBLIC_KEY and RIO_SERVICE_TOKEN.
Nonce Registry: Implement with TTL-based eviction and fail-closed semantics.
Key Fingerprint Stability: The approver field MUST be computed consistently for all entries from the same key.
Emergency Kill Switch: Support EKS-0 as specified in Section 7.4.
Cross-Table Linkage: parameters_hash MUST enable unambiguous cross-reference between execution_ledger and post_execution_ledger.
Model Routing Determinism: Same (model_field, calibrated_intent) pair MUST always select the same model.

8_5_conformance_test_categories.tests: Hash computation test vectors (Appendix C); signature round-trip; DER encoding round-trip.
Submit same signed request twice (HTTP 409). Submit same nonce twice (HTTP 401).
No execution_token -> HTTP 403. RIO_PUBLIC_KEY unset -> HTTP 503. Timestamp >300s old -> HTTP 401.
After N successful requests, verify execution_ledger chain is intact. Modify any entry; verify chain integrity check returns false.
Verify HMAC signatures on post_execution_ledger entries using RIO_SERVICE_TOKEN.
A Guard 1 failure must not produce a Guard 3 error. First failing guard's error MUST be returned.
After a blocked request, verify exactly one execution_ledger row with result='blocked' and one gate_log row with event='unauthorized execution attempt'.

section_9_security.title: 9. Security Considerations

9_1_threat_model.mitigations: Sovereign Gate (ECDSA intent authentication) + Execution Gate (ECDSA token authorization). Both must pass.
Signature Registry (per-signature single-use, disk-persistent) + Nonce Registry (per-nonce single-use, in-memory) + Timestamp freshness window (300s). Three independent defenses.
SHA-256 hash-chain sealing. Modification of any field breaks the chain for all subsequent rows. Post-execution entries additionally carry HMAC-SHA256 signatures.
The signed message is always UTF-8_encode(intent). The execution token canonical bytes include the intent. Substituting a different intent invalidates both signatures.
Fail-closed principle (INV-06). Any infrastructure error blocks execution. No silent fallback.
Atomic check-and-insert in Stage 1 with SQLite UNIQUE constraint. Both the INSERT sequence and the IntegrityError path deny concurrent replays.
RIO_PRIVATE_KEY is loaded only inside signing functions and is never logged, returned in responses, or assigned to module-level variables.

9_4_key_management_requirements: Private key isolation: RIO_PRIVATE_KEY MUST be stored in a secret management system. MUST NOT be stored in source code, config files, or version control.
Key rotation: The key pair SHOULD be rotatable without system downtime. After rotation, approver field in subsequent entries will reflect the new key fingerprint.

HMAC key rotation: RIO_SERVICE_TOKEN rotation invalidates ability to verify HMAC signatures on pre-rotation post_execution_ledger entries. Archive the old token alongside old entries if retrospective verification is required.
Key size: secp256k1 provides approximately 128-bit security.
Nonce generation: Callers MUST use a cryptographically secure random source. Minimum recommended: 128 bits / 32 hex characters.

A_1_intake_request.intent: Summarise the key properties of the secp256k1 elliptic curve in three bullet points.

payload.context: Audience: software engineers implementing cryptographic systems.

A_2_successful_response.response:
The secp256k1 elliptic curve has three key properties:

- Prime field: Defined over a 256-bit prime field, giving 128-bit security.
- Cofactor 1: h=1, meaning every non-identity point generates the full group.
- Efficient endomorphism: Supports a Frobenius endomorphism enabling speedup in scalar multiplication.

A_3_blocked_missing_token.detail: Execution token is required. All execution must be gate-authorized.

B_2_execution_ledger_blocked._description: Blocked — Guard 3 (missing token). receipt_hash is empty string; the | separator and empty string are still present in seal_data for entry_hash computation.

B_3_post_execution_ledger._description: Successful execution. parameters_hash matches B_1 — linking the gate decision to the execution outcome.

C_1_intent_hash.input_string: Summarise the key properties of the secp256k1 elliptic curve in three bullet points.
C_1_intent_hash.verification_note: Compute SHA-256 of the UTF-8 encoding of that exact string and confirm the result matches a known-good SHA-256 implementation.

inputs.intent: Summarise the key properties of the secp256k1 elliptic curve in three bullet points.

C_2_parameters_hash.canonical_string: Summarise the key properties of the secp256k1 elliptic curve in three bullet points.|manus|2026-03-26T14:00:00.000000Z

C_3_entry_hash_blocked.critical_note: Two consecutive || in the seal data — between agent (empty) and approver, and between receipt_hash (empty) and prev_hash. Both empty strings MUST be present.

inputs.intent: Summarise the key properties of the secp256k1 elliptic curve in three bullet points.

C_5_in_memory_receipt_hash.data_template: GENESIS|manus|Summarise the key properties of the secp256k1 elliptic curve in three bullet points.|claude|<response_first_500_chars>|2026-03-26T14:00:03.847221Z

C_7_key_fingerprint.verification: Given a known RIO_PUBLIC_KEY string, compute SHA-256, take the first 16 hex characters, and compare against the approver field in any ledger entry produced by the same gateway configuration.

http_status_codes.contains: response, receipt_hash, ledger_index, signature_hash, signature_verified, model_used, timestamp
